title: Component Object
description: Represents a component in the data model. A component represents a set of geometry, features, and parameters that make up an item in the design. A component can be referenced multiple times into a design with a Occurrence object.

methods.name: allOccurrencesByComponent
methods.description: Returns all occurrences, at any level of the assembly, that reference the specified component. The returned list is read-only.

methods.name: boundingBox2
methods.description: Returns the bounding box of the specified entity types within the component.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createBRepEdgeProfile
methods.description: Creates a profile based on the outside open edges of a BRepFace.

methods.name: createFlatPattern
methods.description: Creates a flat pattern of the sheet metal folded body. The isSheetMetal property of the BRepBody object can be used to determine if a particular body can be flattened. Creating a flat pattern will fail if a flat pattern already exists in the component. You can determine if a flat pattern already exists by checking if the flatPattern property returns a FlatPattern object or null.

methods.name: createOpenProfile
methods.description: Creates an open profile based on the input curve(s).

methods.name: createThumbnail
methods.description: Creates a thumbnail for this component. This can be a root component to get a thumbnail that represents the associated file, or it can be any sub component to get a thumbnail of a subset of the complete assembly or individual parts.

methods.name: findBRepUsingPoint
methods.description: Finds all the entities of the specified type at the specified location.

methods.name: findBRepUsingRay
methods.description: Finds all the B-Rep entities that are intersected by the specified ray. This can return BRepFace, BrepEdge, and BRepVertex objects.

methods.name: getPhysicalProperties
methods.description: Returns the PhysicalProperties object that has properties for getting the area, density, mass, volume, moments, etc of this component.

methods.name: occurrencesByComponent
methods.description: Returns all occurrences at the top-level of this component that reference the specified component. The returned list is read-only.

methods.name: saveCopyAs
methods.description: Performs a Save Copy As on this component. This saves the specified component as a new document in the specified location.

methods.name: transformOccurrences
methods.description: Transforms a set of occurrences in one step. This provides better performance than transforming them one at a time. This method is only valid when called on the root component because Fusion flattens the entire assembly structure when manipulating the assembly so all transforms are relative to the root component.

properties.name: activeSheetMetalRule
properties.description: Gets and sets the active sheet metal rule. This can return null in the case where the component has never contained any sheet metal related data.

properties.name: allAsBuiltJoints
properties.description: Returns all joint origins in this component and any sub components. The joint origins returned are all in the context of this component so any joint origins in sub components will be proxies. This is primarily useful when used from the root component because Fusion flattens the assembly structure, including joint origins, when manipulating an assembly.

properties.name: allAssemblyConstraints
properties.description: Returns all assembly constraints in this component and any sub components. The assembly constraints returned are all in the context of this component so any joints in sub components will be proxies. This is primarily useful when used from the root component because Fusion flattens the assembly structure, including joints, when manipulating an assembly.

properties.name: allJointOrigins
properties.description: Returns all as-built joints in this component and any sub components. The as-built joints returned are all in the context of this component so any as-built joints in sub components will be proxies. This is primarily useful when used from the root component because Fusion flattens the assembly structure, including as-built joints, when manipulating an assembly.

properties.name: allJoints
properties.description: Returns all joints in this component and any sub components. The joints returned are all in the context of this component so any joints in sub components will be proxies. This is primarily useful when used from the root component because Fusion flattens the assembly structure, including joints, when manipulating an assembly.

properties.name: allOccurrences
properties.description: Returns all of the occurrences in the assembly regardless of their level within the assembly structure. The returned list is read-only.

properties.name: allRigidGroups
properties.description: Returns all rigid groups in this component and any sub components. The rigid groups returned are all in the context of this component so any rigid groups in sub components will be proxies. This is primarily useful when used from the root component because Fusion flattens the assembly structure, including rigid groups, when manipulating an assembly.

properties.name: allTangentRelationships
properties.description: Returns all tangent relationships in this component and any sub components. The tangent relationships returned are all in the context of this component so any tangent relationships in sub components will be proxies. This is primarily useful when used from the root component because Fusion flattens the assembly structure, including tangent relationships, when manipulating an assembly.

properties.name: asBuiltJoints
properties.description: Returns the collection of as-built joints associated with this component.

properties.name: assemblyConstraints
properties.description: Returns the collection of assembly constraints associated with this component.

properties.name: attributes
properties.description: Returns the collection of attributes associated with this face.

properties.name: boundingBox
properties.description: Returns the bounding box of this component. This is always in world space of the component. The boundingBox2 method provides greater control over which types of entities you want included in the bounding box calculation.

properties.name: bRepBodies
properties.description: Returns the B-Rep bodies collection associated with this component.

properties.name: canvases
properties.description: Returns the canvases collection associated with this component. This provides access to the existing canvases and supports the creation of new canvases.

properties.name: componentColor
properties.description: Gets and sets the color associated with a component. This color is only used when the "Display Component Colors" command is enabled. Enabling the display of component colors is done through the command or API using the Application.isComponentColorsDisplayed property. When this is on, all bodies in a component will display using the color assigned to the component. Fusion randomly assigns a color to a component when it is created. This property allows you to get and change the assigned default color. Setting this property to null results in a new color being randomly assigned by Fusion. This is the equivalent of the "Cycle Component Color" command available in the context menu of a component.

properties.name: constructionAxes
properties.description: Returns the construction axes collection associated with this component. This provides access to the existing construction axes and supports the creation of new construction axes.

properties.name: constructionPlanes
properties.description: Returns the construction planes collection associated with this component. This provides access to the existing construction planes and supports the creation of new construction planes.

properties.name: constructionPoints
properties.description: Returns the construction points collection associated with this component. This provides access to the existing construction points and supports the creation of new construction points.

properties.name: customGraphicsGroups
properties.description: Returns the customGraphicsGroups object in this component.

properties.name: dataComponent
properties.description: Returns the DataComponent associated with this component. The DataComponent provides ID information that can be used to access this component using the MFG DM API. These ID's don't exist until a component has been saved. The ID's are generated by MFG DM API on the cloud, so there will be a slight delay after saving before the ID's are available. This property returns null in the case the MFG DM API information doesn't exist yet.   When opening a design, the MFG DM API information is obtained from the cloud and as a result may not be available immediately after opening a document. Again, this property will return null in this case too. Essentially, null is returned in all cases where good ID information is not yet available.

properties.name: decals
properties.description: Returns the decals collection associated with this component. This provides access to the existing decals and supports the creation of new decals.

properties.name: description
properties.description: Gets and sets the description associated with this component.

properties.name: entityToken
properties.description: Returns a token for the Component object. This can be saved and used at a later time with the Design.findEntityByToken method to get back the same component.   When using entity tokens it's important to understand that the token string returned for a specific entity can be different over time. However, even if you have two different token strings that were obtained from the same entity, when you use findEntityByToken they will both return the same entity. Because of that you should never compare entity tokens as way to determine what the token represents. Instead, you need to use the findEntityByToken method to get the two entities identified by the tokens and then compare them.

properties.name: features
properties.description: Returns the collection that provides access to all of the features associated with this component.

properties.name: flatPattern
properties.description: Gets the existing flat pattern or returns null in the case where a flat pattern doesn't exist in this component.

properties.name: id
properties.description: Returns the persistent ID of the component. This ID is created with the component and does not change. Because this ID does not change, different revisions of the same design or copies of the design asset/file will retain this ID. If components from different designs have the same ID, it indicates they are either different revisions or a copy of the design was made. Therefore, this ID will always be unique within a single design, but may not be unique in an assembly where externally referenced designs include different revisions or copies of a design.   The ID is also the same ID used by PIM (Product Information Model).

properties.name: isBodiesFolderLightBulbOn
properties.description: Gets and sets if the light bulb of the bodies folder as seen in the browser is on or off. This controls the visibility of the solid/surface bodies and the mesh bodies in this component.

properties.name: isCanvasFolderLightBulbOn
properties.description: Gets and sets if the light bulb of the canvas folder as seen in the browser is on or off. This controls the visibility of all the canvases in the component.

properties.name: isConstructionFolderLightBulbOn
properties.description: Gets and sets if the light bulb of the construction folder as seen in the browser is on or off. This controls the visibility of the (non-origin) construction geometry (i.e. planes, points, axes).

properties.name: isDecalFolderLightBulbOn
properties.description: Gets and sets whether the light bulb of the decal folder, as seen in the browser, is on or off. This controls the visibility of all the decals in the component.

properties.name: isJointOriginsFolderLightBulbOn
properties.description: Gets and sets if the light bulb of the joint origins folder as seen in the browser is on or off. This controls the visibility of the joint origins in this occurrence. The light bulb for the folder is component specific and will turn off the joints for all occurrences referencing the component.

properties.name: isJointsFolderLightBulbOn
properties.description: Gets and sets if the light bulb of the joints folder as seen in the browser is on or off. This controls the visibility of the joints in this occurrence. The light bulb for the folder is component specific and will turn off the joints for all occurrences referencing the component.

properties.name: isLibraryItem
properties.description: Gets whether the component was created from a fasteners library item."

properties.name: isOriginFolderLightBulbOn
properties.description: Gets and sets if the light bulb of the origin folder as seen in the browser is on or off. This controls the visibility of the origin construction geometry.

properties.name: isSketchFolderLightBulbOn
properties.description: Gets and sets if the light bulb of the sketch folder as seen in the browser is on or off. This controls the visibility of the sketches in this component.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: jointOrigins
properties.description: Returns the collection of joint origins associated with this component.

properties.name: joints
properties.description: Returns the collection of joints associated with this component.

properties.name: material
properties.description: Gets and sets the physical material assigned to this component.

properties.name: meshBodies
properties.description: Returns the mesh bodies collection associated with this component.

properties.name: mfgdmModelId
properties.description: Returns the MFGDM model ID for this component.

properties.name: modelParameters
properties.description: Returns the collection of model parameters in the Component.

properties.name: motionLinks
properties.description: Returns the collection of MotionLinks associated with this component.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Property that gets and sets the name of this component. This is the name shown in the browser for each occurrence referencing this component.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: occurrences
properties.description: Property that returns the Occurrences collection associated with this component. This provides access to the occurrences at the top-level of this component and provides the functionality to add new occurrences.

properties.name: opacity
properties.description: Gets and sets the opacity override assigned to this component. A value of 1.0 specifies that is it completely opaque and a value of 0.0 specifies that is it completely transparent.   This is only applicable for a non-root local component.   This value is not necessarily related to what the user sees because the opacity is inherited. For example, if you have TopComponent and it has a component in it called SubComponent and you set the opacity of TopComponent to be 0.5, SubComponent will also be shown as slightly transparent even though the opacity property for it will return 1.0. Because a component can be referenced as an occurrence in other components and they can have different opacity settings, it's possible that different instances of the same component can display using different opacity levels. To get the opacity that it is being displayed with use the Occurrence.visibleOpacity property.

properties.name: orientedMinimumBoundingBox
properties.description: Returns an oriented bounding box that is best oriented to tightly fit all B-Rep bodies in the component. All other geometry types are ignored.

properties.name: originConstructionPoint
properties.description: Returns the origin construction point.

properties.name: parentDesign
properties.description: Returns the parent product this component is owned by.

properties.name: partNumber
properties.description: Gets and sets the part number associated with this component. Setting this to an empty string will reset it to be the same as the component name.

properties.name: physicalProperties
properties.description: Returns the PhysicalProperties object that has properties for getting the area, density, mass, volume, moments, etc of this component. Property values will be calculated using the 'LowCalculationAccuracy' setting when using this property to get the PhysicalProperties object. To specify a higher calculation tolerance, use the getPhysicalProperties method instead.

properties.name: preciseBoundingBox
properties.description: Returns a bounding box that tightly fits around all B-Rep bodies in the component. All other geometry types are ignored.

properties.name: propertyGroups
properties.description: Returns the PropertyGroups object associated with this component.

properties.name: revisionId
properties.description: Returns the current revision ID of the component. This ID changes any time the component is modified in any way. By getting and saving the ID when you create any data that is dependent on the component, you can then compare the saved ID with the current ID to determine if the component has changed to know if you should update your data.

properties.name: rigidGroups
properties.description: Returns the collection of rigid groups associated with this component.

properties.name: sketches
properties.description: Returns the sketches collection associated with this component. This provides access to the existing sketches and supports the creation of new sketches.

properties.name: tangentRelationships
properties.description: Returns the collection of tangent relationships associated with this component.

properties.name: xConstructionAxis
properties.description: Returns the X origin construction axis.

properties.name: xYConstructionPlane
properties.description: Returns the XY origin construction plane.

properties.name: xZConstructionPlane
properties.description: Returns the XZ origin construction plane.

properties.name: yConstructionAxis
properties.description: Returns the Y origin construction axis.

properties.name: yZConstructionPlane
properties.description: Returns the YZ origin construction plane.

properties.name: zConstructionAxis
properties.description: Returns the Z origin construction axis.

related_samples.name: Create Setups From Hole Recognition API Sample
related_samples.description: This sample script demonstrates how to create a correctly oriented setup using Hole Recognition functionality.The Fusion Manufacturing Extension is required for Hole Recognition.The script starts by opening a sample model from the CAM Samples folder via its URN. The model comprises a 3 way coupling containing holes in various orientations and mounted on a fixture. A reference setup is created for the model using a simple stock mode and offsets. The Hole Recognition feature of the Fusion Manufacturing Extension creates 5 hole groups containing 11 holes between them. For each unique hole group vector captured, a new setup is created and its orientation transformed to match the vector.

related_samples.name: Library Item API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to examine library items using the API.
To use the sample, create a new Python or C++ script and copy and paste this code, replacing the existing default code. The script will search for and record all components and library items in the current project. They are displayed in a dialog when the script has finished.

related_samples.name: Loft Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new loft feature.

related_samples.name: Set Vise Origin As Setup WCS Origin API Sample
related_samples.description: This sample script demonstrates how to define our setup WCS origin relative to our vise origin from either a component, a sketch point or a joint origin.The Work Coordinate System is a reference point set in our CAM workspace and on our machine. All machine coordinates are drawn from the WCS. This script demonstrates defining the WCS by each of 4 alternative methods:Setup by default with no origin defined.Setup using vise origin as WCS origin.Setup using a sketch point as WCS origin.Setup using a joint origin as WCS origin.

activeSheetMetalRule.title: Component.activeSheetMetalRule Property
activeSheetMetalRule.description: Gets and sets the active sheet metal rule. This can return null in the case where the component has never contained any sheet metal related data.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = component_var.activeSheetMetalRule

# Set the value of the property.
component_var.activeSheetMetalRule = propertyValue

related_samples.name: Rendering Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates using the Rendering capabilities in the API. This starts a series of local renderings to generate a series of frames, where the camera is repositioned and a parameter is modified for each frame to create a dynamic animation. To use the sample, have a design open that contains a parameter named "Length". It can be a user or model parameter. The sample will modify this parameter from a value of 0.1 cm to 15 cm, but you can change these values by editing the values of the paramStartVal and paramEndVal variables on lines 90 and 91 of the sample. It expects a folder named "C:\Temp\RenderSample" to exist, and will fail if it doesn't. The rendered frames will be written to that folder.An example rendering is shown below where this file was used. The parameter is modifying a move feature which results in changing the shape of an extrusion. The parameter could be driving anything and you could modify the code to edit more than one parameter. The result of this sample is a folder containing all of the rendered frames. You can process these to create an animation. The sample animation was created using GIMP.

canvases.title: Component.canvases Property
canvases.description: Returns the canvases collection associated with this component. This provides access to the existing canvases and supports the creation of new canvases.

syntax.python:

componentColor.title: Component.componentColor Property
componentColor.description: Gets and sets the color associated with a component. This color is only used when the "Display Component Colors" command is enabled. Enabling the display of component colors is done through the command or API using the Application.isComponentColorsDisplayed property. When this is on, all bodies in a component will display using the color assigned to the component. Fusion randomly assigns a color to a component when it is created. This property allows you to get and change the assigned default color. Setting this property to null results in a new color being randomly assigned by Fusion. This is the equivalent of the "Cycle Component Color" command available in the context menu of a component.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = component_var.componentColor

# Set the value of the property.
component_var.componentColor = propertyValue

To use the sample, create a new Python or C++ script and copy and paste this code, replacing the default code. You also need to unpack this zip file which contains a resource folder into the same folder where the source code file (.py or .cpp) is.

id.title: Component.id Property
id.description: Returns the persistent ID of the component. This ID is created with the component and does not change. Because this ID does not change, different revisions of the same design or copies of the design asset/file will retain this ID. If components from different designs have the same ID, it indicates they are either different revisions or a copy of the design was made. Therefore, this ID will always be unique within a single design, but may not be unique in an assembly where externally referenced designs include different revisions or copies of a design. The ID is also the same ID used by PIM (Product Information Model).

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = component_var.id

related_samples.name: Set Vise Origin As Setup WCS Origin API Sample
related_samples.description: This sample script demonstrates how to define our setup WCS origin relative to our vise origin from either a component, a sketch point or a joint origin.The Work Coordinate System is a reference point set in our CAM workspace and on our machine. All machine coordinates are drawn from the WCS. This script demonstrates defining the WCS by each of 4 alternative methods:Setup by default with no origin defined.Setup using vise origin as WCS origin.Setup using a sketch point as WCS origin.Setup using a joint origin as WCS origin.

objectType.title: Component.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = component_var.objectType

occurrences.description: Property that returns the Occurrences collection associated with this component. This provides access to the occurrences at the top-level of this component and provides the functionality to add new occurrences.

related_samples.name: Assembly traversal using recursion API Sample
related_samples.description: Traverses the entire structure of the currently open assemlby using a recursive function and displays the result in a message box. This will match the occurrence structure seen in the browser.

opacity.title: Component.opacity Property
opacity.description: Gets and sets the opacity override assigned to this component. A value of 1.0 specifies that is it completely opaque and a value of 0.0 specifies that is it completely transparent. This is only applicable for a non-root local component. This value is not necessarily related to what the user sees because the opacity is inherited. For example, if you have TopComponent and it has a component in it called SubComponent and you set the opacity of TopComponent to be 0.5, SubComponent will also be shown as slightly transparent even though the opacity property for it will return 1.0. Because a component can be referenced as an occurrence in other components and they can have different opacity settings, it's possible that different instances of the same component can display using different opacity levels. To get the opacity that it is being displayed with use the Occurrence.visibleOpacity property.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = component_var.opacity

# Set the value of the property.
component_var.opacity = propertyValue

orientedMinimumBoundingBox.title: Component.orientedMinimumBoundingBox Property
orientedMinimumBoundingBox.description: Returns an oriented bounding box that is best oriented to tightly fit all B-Rep bodies in the component. All other geometry types are ignored.

related_samples.name: Create Setups From Hole Recognition API Sample
related_samples.description: This sample script demonstrates how to create a correctly oriented setup using Hole Recognition functionality.The Fusion Manufacturing Extension is required for Hole Recognition.The script starts by opening a sample model from the CAM Samples folder via its URN. The model comprises a 3 way coupling containing holes in various orientations and mounted on a fixture. A reference setup is created for the model using a simple stock mode and offsets. The Hole Recognition feature of the Fusion Manufacturing Extension creates 5 hole groups containing 11 holes between them. For each unique hole group vector captured, a new setup is created and its orientation transformed to match the vector.

All machine coordinates are drawn from the WCS. syntax.cpp:

boundingBox2.return_description: Returns a BoundingBox3D object if the calculation was successful and null in the case where there is no valid geometry and the bounding box is empty.

parameters.name: entityTypes
parameters.description: Bitwise value that specifies the types of entities to include in the calculation of the bounding box.

classType.title: Component.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.fusion.Component.classType()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::fusion::Component::classType();

classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createBRepEdgeProfile.title: Component.createBRepEdgeProfile Method
createBRepEdgeProfile.description: Creates a profile based on the outside open edges of a BRepFace.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = component_var.createBRepEdgeProfile(edges)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = component_var.createBRepEdgeProfile(edges, chainEdges)

createBRepEdgeProfile.return_description: Returns the new Profile object or null in the case of a failure.

parameters.name: edges
parameters.description: A single BRepEdge object or an ObjectCollection containing multiple BRepEdge objects, or a BRepLoop object. If a single edge is input, the chainEdges argument is checked to determine if connected edges (they do not need to be tangent) should be automatically found. If multiple edges are provided the chainEdges argument is always treated as false so you must provide all of the edges in the object collection that you want included in the profile. and the edges must all connect together in a single path. if a BRepLoop object is provided, all of the edges in the loop are included in the profile and the chainEdges argument is ignored.

parameters.name: chainEdges
parameters.description: If true, this finds any edges that connect to the single input edge and automatically includes them in the profile. If false, only the edges provided will be used to define the profile. This argument is ignored and treated as false if multiple edges or a BRepLoop is input.   This is an optional argument whose default value is True.

createFlatPattern.title: Component.createFlatPattern Method
createFlatPattern.description: Creates a flat pattern of the sheet metal folded body. The isSheetMetal property of the BRepBody object can be used to determine if a particular body can be flattened. Creating a flat pattern will fail if a flat pattern already exists in the component. You can determine if a flat pattern already exists by checking if the flatPattern property returns a FlatPattern object or null.

syntax.python: returnValue = component_var.createFlatPattern(stationaryFace)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = component_var->createFlatPattern(stationaryFace);

createFlatPattern.return_description: Returns the newly created flat pattern.

parameters.name: stationaryFace
parameters.description: A planar face in the sheet metal body that is on the top or bottom of the part and not an edge face. This face will be positioned on the X-Y plane of the flat pattern and the rest of the model will be flattened relative to this face. The face must exist on a body that is owned by this component.

createOpenProfile.title: Component.createOpenProfile Method
createOpenProfile.description: Creates an open profile based on the input curve(s).

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = component_var.createOpenProfile(curves)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = component_var.createOpenProfile(curves, chainCurves)

createOpenProfile.return_description: Returns the new Profile object or null in the case of a failure.

parameters.name: curves
parameters.description: A SketchCurve or an ObjectCollection containing multiple sketch entities. If a single sketch curve is input the chainCurves argument is checked to determine if connected curves (they do not need to be tangent) should be automatically found. If multiple curves are provided the chainCurves argument is always treated as false so you must provide all of the curves in the object collection that you want included in the profile. The provided curves must all connect together in a single path.   The input curves do not need to be in the same sketch, but they do need to geometrically connect for a valid profile to be created.

parameters.name: chainCurves
parameters.description: If true, this finds any curves within the same sketch that connect to the single input curve and automatically includes them in the profile. If false, only the curves provided will be used to define the profile. This argument is ignored and treated as false if multiple curves are input.   This is an optional argument whose default value is True.

createThumbnail.title: Component.createThumbnail Method
createThumbnail.description: Creates a thumbnail for this component. This can be a root component to get a thumbnail that represents the associated file, or it can be any sub component to get a thumbnail of a subset of the complete assembly or individual parts.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = component_var.createThumbnail()

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = component_var.createThumbnail(width, height, imageType)

createThumbnail.return_description: Returns a DataObject that you can use to save the thumbnail to a file or to access the binary data of the bitmap directly.

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: Optional argument to define the width of the thumbnail in pixels. A default width of 256 pixels is used if no width is specified.   This is an optional argument whose default value is 256.

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: Optional argument to define the height of the thumbnail in pixels. A default height of 256 pixels is used if no height is specified.   This is an optional argument whose default value is 256.

parameters.name: imageType
parameters.description: Optional argument to define the type of image data to create. The default of PNG is used if no type is specified. Valid types include "PNG", "JPG", "TIF", and "BMP".   This is an optional argument whose default value is "PNG".

findBRepUsingPoint.title: Component.findBRepUsingPoint Method
findBRepUsingPoint.description: Finds all the entities of the specified type at the specified location.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = component_var.findBRepUsingPoint(point, entityType)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = component_var.findBRepUsingPoint(point, entityType, proximityTolerance, visibleEntitiesOnly)

findBRepUsingPoint.return_description: Returns an ObjectCollection containing the entities found. The returned collection can be empty indicating nothing was found.

parameters.name: point
parameters.description: Input coordinate that specifies the component space point at which to find the entities.

parameters.name: entityType
parameters.description: The type of B-Rep entity wanted. You can also take advantage of B-Rep topology to infer other that other entities were found. For example, If you get a BRepEdge it implies that the faces the edge connects were also found. If a BRepVertex is returned it implies the edges that the vertex connects were found and the faces that the edges connect were found.

parameters.name: proximityTolerance
parameters.description: Specifies the tolerance for the search. All entities within this distance from the search point that match the filter will be returned. If not specified a default tolerance is used.   This is an optional argument whose default value is -1.0.

parameters.name: visibleEntitiesOnly
parameters.description: indicates whether or not invisible objects should be included in the search. Defaults to True indicating that invisible objects will be ignored.   This is an optional argument whose default value is True.

related_samples.name: Hole and Pocket Recognition API Sample
related_samples.description: This sample script demonstrates three different methods for feature recognition: one for holes and two for pockets.The script starts by creating a simple component which is then used to demonstrate the three methods. After the features are recognised they are coloured and milling and drilling operations are created for each feature.RecognizedHoleGroup returns a list of BRepFaces that can be used as selections for the drilling operation. RecognizedPocket also return some BRepFaces but it needs additional processing before the output can be used for creating machining operations.The sample script demonstrates a couple of different methods, including finding the pocket BRepFaces and creating sketches from the recognized pockets.This script works only if the Manufacturing Extension is active.

findBRepUsingRay.title: Component.findBRepUsingRay Method
findBRepUsingRay.description: Finds all the B-Rep entities that are intersected by the specified ray. This can return BRepFace, BrepEdge, and BRepVertex objects.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = component_var.findBRepUsingRay(originPoint, rayDirection, entityType)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = component_var.findBRepUsingRay(originPoint, rayDirection, entityType, proximityTolerance, visibleEntitiesOnly, hitPoints)

findBRepUsingRay.return_description: Returns an ObjectCollection containing the entities found. The returned collection can be empty indicating nothing was found. The points are returned in an order where they are arranged based on their distance from the origin point where the closest point is first. If an entity is hit more than once, the entity is returned once for the first intersection.

parameters.name: originPoint
parameters.description: Input point that defines the origin of the ray. The search for entities begins at this point.

parameters.name: rayDirection
parameters.description: Input vector that defines the direction of the ray. The ray is infinite so the length of the vector is ignored.

parameters.name: entityType
parameters.description: The type of B-Rep entity wanted. You can also take advantage of B-Rep topology to infer other intersections. For example, If you get a BRepEdge it implies that the faces the edge connects were also intersected. If a BRepVertex is returned it implies the edges that the vertex connects were intersected and the faces that the edges connect were intersected.

parameters.name: proximityTolerance
parameters.description: Optional argument that specifies the tolerance for the search. All entities within this distance from the ray and of the specified type will be returned. If not specified a default small tolerance is used.   This is an optional argument whose default value is -1.0.

parameters.name: visibleEntitiesOnly
parameters.description: Optional argument that indicates whether or not invisible entities should be included in the search. Defaults to True indicating that invisible entities will be ignored.   This is an optional argument whose default value is True.

parameters.name: hitPoints
parameters.description: An ObjectCollection of Point3D objects that represent the coordinates where the ray hit the found entity. There will be the same number of hit points as returned entities and they will be in the collections in the same order. In other words, hit point 1 corresponds with found entity 1, hit point 2 corresponds with found entity 2, and so on. Because of the proximity tolerance the hitPoint may not actually lie on the entity but will be within the proximity tolerance to it. It's an optional out argument, returns the hit points if an existing ObjectCollection is input. You can create a new ObjectCollection by using the static create method on the ObjectCollection class.   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

getPhysicalProperties.title: Component.getPhysicalProperties Method
getPhysicalProperties.description: Returns the PhysicalProperties object that has properties for getting the area, density, mass, volume, moments, etc of this component.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = component_var.getPhysicalProperties()

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = component_var.getPhysicalProperties(accuracy)

getPhysicalProperties.return_description: Returns a PhysicalProperties object that can be used to get the various physical property related values.

parameters.name: accuracy
parameters.description: Specifies the desired level of computational accuracy of the property calculations. The default value of 'LowCalculationAccuracy' returns results within a +/- 1% error margin.   This is an optional argument whose default value is CalculationAccuracy.LowCalculationAccuracy.

related_samples.name: Get Physical Properties API Sample
related_samples.description: Script that demonstrates getting physical properties using the API. When this script is run it will create a new document, build a simple model, and get the various physical properties from the model.

occurrencesByComponent.title: Component.occurrencesByComponent Method
occurrencesByComponent.description: Returns all occurrences at the top-level of this component that reference the specified component. The returned list is read-only.

syntax.python: returnValue = component_var.occurrencesByComponent(component)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = component_var->occurrencesByComponent(component);

occurrencesByComponent.return_description: The occurrences referenced by the specified component.

parameters.name: component
parameters.description: The component that is being referenced by the occurrences that will be returned.

saveCopyAs.title: Component.saveCopyAs Method
saveCopyAs.description: Performs a Save Copy As on this component. This saves the specified component as a new document in the specified location.

syntax.python: returnValue = component_var.saveCopyAs(name, dataFolder, description, tag)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = component_var->saveCopyAs(name, dataFolder, description, tag);

saveCopyAs.return_description: Returns a DataFileFuture object that can be used to track the progress of the upload and get the resulting DataFile once it's available on A360.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name to use for the new document. If this is an empty string, Fusion will use the name of the component being saved.

parameters.name: dataFolder
parameters.description: The data folder to save the new document to.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: The description string of the document. This can be an empty string.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: The tag string of the document. This can be an empty string.

transformOccurrences.title: Component.transformOccurrences Method
transformOccurrences.description: Transforms a set of occurrences in one step. This provides better performance than transforming them one at a time. This method is only valid when called on the root component because Fusion flattens the entire assembly structure when manipulating the assembly so all transforms are relative to the root component.

syntax.python: returnValue = component_var.transformOccurrences(occurrences, transforms, ignoreJoints)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = component_var->transformOccurrences(occurrences, transforms, ignoreJoints);

transformOccurrences.return_description: Returns true if the transform was successful.

parameters.name: occurrences
parameters.description: An array of Occurrence objects that you want to transform. These must all be in the context of the root component which means proxies must be used for occurrences that are in sub-components.

parameters.name: transforms
parameters.description: An array of Matrix3D objects that defines the transform to apply to each occurrence. This array must be the same size as the array provided for the occurrences argument and the transform will be applied to the occurrence at the same index in the occurrences array.

parameters.name: ignoreJoints
parameters.description: Specifies if the joints are to be ignored and the occurrences are to be positioned based on then specified transform or if the joints should be used and the occurrence is transformed the best it can while still honoring the joints.
